steps.title: Turn the facts into points
steps.content: A linear profit function is determined by two points. The profit is $170$ when $x=4$ and $590$ when $x=10$, so use $(4,170)$ and $(10,590)$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: Use the two points directly. From $(4,170)$ to $(10,590)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{590-170}{10-4}=70.$$ Substitute $(4,170)$ into $y=70x+b$: $$170=70(4)+b$$ $$b=-110$$ So $f(x)=70x-110$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D is correct. It checks both points: $70(4)-110=170$ and $70(10)-110=590$. The other choices fail at least one of those two point checks.
